book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon IX.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: As many as have not merely apostatized, but have risen against their brethren and forced them [to apostatize], and have been guilty of their being forced, let these for three years take the place of hearers, and for another term of six years that of prostrators, and for another year let them communicate without oblation, in order that, when they have fulfilled the space of ten years, they may partake of the communion; but during this time the rest of their life must also be enquired into.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Whoever has not only sacrificed voluntarily but also has forced another to sacrifice, shall be a prostrator for ten years.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: From this canon we are taught that the circumstances of the sin that has been committed are to be taken into account in assigning the penance.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: When the ten years are past, he is worthy of perfection, and fit to receive the divine sacraments.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Unless perchance an examination of the rest of his life demands his exclusion from the divine communion.
